board.title: Computer Vision
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Computer vision emerged in the 1960s as a subfield of artificial intelligence. The field draws heavily on mathematics, physics, and signal processing. Today, it powers applications such as medical imaging, satellite imagery, and robotics.

clues.question: Image processing operation that slides filters across an image for edge detection, blurring, or texture extraction
Technique for estimating the apparent motion between consecutive images caused by object or camera movement
Transformation that maps 3D points onto a 2D image
Technique for estimating depth using two viewpoints from slightly different angles, similar to human binocular vision
Task of partitioning an image into regions by assigning a class label to each pixel
Task of identifying and following objects across consecutive images
Camera model that projects a 3D scene onto a 2D image through a single opening
Technique for estimating a camera’s parameters from known reference points such as a checkerboard for accurate geometric measurements
Foundational edge detection algorithm for identifying object boundaries while suppressing noise; introduced by John Canny in 1986
Task of categorizing an entire image by assigning it a class label
Method for detecting geometric shapes such as lines and curves by accumulating votes across a parameter space; introduced by Paul V. C. Hough in 1962
Transformation for mapping one view of a plane to another, such as stitching panoramic images
Method for estimating transformations between images by finding consensus among randomly sampled matching points
Method for detecting and describing scale-invariant and rotation-invariant features in images
Neural network architecture for image classification, detection, and segmentation; helped popularize deep learning for computer vision
Task of locating objects within an image and assigning each a class label
